rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Injunctive Relief Against Pltf Loran Simon For A Court Order Of Mandatory Injunction Ordering Plaintiff Loran Simon To Cancel Expired Permit And To Allow Compliance With Fire Ordinance; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 26, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT 426 FILLMORE ASSOCATION Motion For Injunctive Relief Against Pltf Loran Simon For A Court Order Of Mandatory Injunction Ordering Plaintiff Loran Simon To Cancel Expired Permit And To Allow Compliance With Fire Ordinance; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to carry its burden as each of the injunctions requested. Should the moving party re-file the motion for preliminary injunction, the moving papers must identify (1) case authority addressing injunctions granted to defendants; and/or (2) support each element of CCP 526(a)(3) including the requirements for acts respecting the subject of the action and acts tending to render the judgment ineffectual. Counsel for the moving party is ordered to familiarize themselves with the San Francisco Local Rules, which can be located on the Court's web-site. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FUNG LEE VS. ANNIE O'KEEFFE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 26, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT ANNIE O'KEEFFE, MICHAEL MOORE DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is OVERRULED as to (1) cause of action for breach of contract, there is no other action pending involving the same claim and the same parties. The claims for unlawful detainer and for breach of contract are not the same; the Plaintiffs, Albert Lee as successor Trustee and Fung Lee, an individual are not the same. The Court notes that two separate plaintiffs claim ownership in the same property and do not allege to be co-owners. This issue was not raised by the Defendants. (2) Cause of action for declaratory relief. Defendant abandoned its arguments having not addressed this cause of action in reply. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to causes of action for fraud, elder abuse and UCL violation with leave to amend to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each element of these causes of action with requisite specificity. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action for CC 3345 with leave to amend to be re-alleged within an appropriate cause of action as relief/request for damages. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E. OSGOOD III ET AL VS. 10 GLENDALE, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Justin M. Marvisi
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 26, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT 10 GLENDALE, LP, GENERAL PARTNER E, LLC, GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Justin M. Marvisi is GRANTED with final leave to amend to allege fact, not conclusions to support (1) punitive damages (2) against corporate defendants. Conclusory statements (e.g, "inadequate steps," "done with intent," etc) and allegations on "information and belief" without any facts regarding the information that caused the belief are insufficient. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FELL HOLDINGS LLC VS. FELL ST AUTOMOTIVE CLINIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File 1st Amended Complaint / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File 1st Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of M. Ryan Pinkston
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 26, 2024 line 12. PLAINTIFF 1215 FELL SF OWNER LLC MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File 1st Amended Complaint / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File 1st Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of M. Ryan Pinkston is DENIED without prejudice. If plaintiff wishes to proceed with this action as an action for unlawful detainer based on any grounds enumerated in CCP 1159 et seq, (CCP 1161 mentioned the Proposed First Amended Complaint without any specific subsection referenced) Plaintiff may file a motion for leave to amend to state such a cause of action (Plaintiff may also include any corrections of typographical errors with explanations for corrections; and any new defendants if they are believed to be in possession of the premises or claim a right to possession). If Plaintiff wishes to proceed with its claims for possession and damages stemming from breach of contract/stipulation, Plaintiff may seek leave of Court to reclassify this action as a general civil action. Plaintiff may not proceed with an unlawful detainer (a summary proceeding with 5-day response time-line and a trial set on 20 day notice) and a civil claim (subject to case management and trial setting out of the Presiding Judge's department) in one action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
